subject: Computer Networking
title: Post-Quantum Cryptography
description: A micro-lesson that makes Post-Quantum Cryptography usable.

hook.text: Quantum computers will break RSA and ECC. Every secure connection on the internet is at risk.

buildup.text: Shor's algorithm factors large numbers fast on quantum hardware. RSA depends on that being hard.

discovery.text: NIST standardized four post-quantum algorithms in 2024. They resist quantum on classical hardware.

twist.text: 'Harvest now, decrypt later' is real. Adversaries store traffic to crack when quantum arrives.

climax.text: Chrome and Signal ship post-quantum key exchange. Billions of systems still need the upgrade.

punchline.text: Quantum will break today's crypto. The migration can't wait.

quiz.question: What is a 'harvest now, decrypt later' attack?
options: Storing encrypted data now to decrypt it when quantum computers exist
Stealing encryption keys from servers in real time
Using quantum computers to break encryption instantly
